FOR PLAINTIFF; JOHN RANUCCI, ESQ. FOR DEFENDANT. ARGUED AND THE COURT GRANTED THE MOTION. THE TRIAL DATE OF 02/22/21 IS CONTINUED TO 06/01/21 IN DEPT. 206 AT 9:30 AM. THE COURT WILL NOT PERMIT ANY FURTHER CONTINUANCES. ALL DISCOVERY IS CLOSED, TRACKING THE TRIAL DATE OF 02/22/21. HOWEVER ANY DISCOVERY THAT WAS NOTICED BEFORE THE 30 DAY CUT-OFF FROM 02/22/21 REMAINS. PARTIES MAY APPEAR BY COURTCALL. ORDER SIGNED IN OPEN COURT. MR. RANUCCI TO SERVE ORDER. PARTIES WAIVED NOTICE. MINI MINUTES:

Attys. STEVEN J. BRADY, Esq. and JESSICA DEWITT, Esq. of Brady Law Group, Inc. (Tel: 415-459-7300) for plaintiff Darcie Layee;
Atty. JOHN W. RANUCCI, Esq. of Rankin, Shuey, Ranucci, Mintz, Lampasona & Reynolds (Tel: 510-433-2600) for Defendant Bogdan-Cristian Fratila.

HEARING RE: DEFENDANT BOGDAN-CRISTIAN FRATILA's Motion To Reopen Discovery.

Both sides stipulate to the matter being decided by the Judge Pro Tem. The Judge Pro Tem and counsel for both sides authorize the clerk to sign the formal stipulation form on their behalf.

Matter argued and submitted. The Court rules as follows: Motion denied based on plaintiff's agreement to withdraw the expert witness.

Counsel for plaintiff is instructed to email a proposed order directly to the Judge Pro Tem at klgallo@discoveryreferee.com.

The report and recommendation of the judge pro tem is adopted. Defendants' motion to compel plaintiff's further responses to requests for admissions is denied.
